independent_tests.test_output_summary: Independent test verification skipped (tests not required for scaffolding tasks)

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `schema.sql` contains `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS builds (...) STRICT`
criteria_verification.evidence: src/forge/lifecycle/schema.sql §2.1 reproduces every column from API-sqlite-schema.md §2.1 (build_id, feature_id, repo, branch, feature_yaml_path, project, status, triggered_by, originating_adapter, originating_user, correlation_id, parent_request_id, queued_at, started_at, completed_at, worktree_path, pr_url, error, max_turns, sdk_timeout_seconds) and adds the F4 recovery column `pending_approval_request_id TEXT`. The CREATE TABLE statement ends with the STRICT marker. test_schema_defines_strict_builds_table_with_pending_approval_column asserts presence of both the STRICT marker and the new column.
criteria_verification.notes: src/forge/lifecycle/schema.sql §2.1 reproduces every column from API-sqlite-schema.md §2.1 (build_id, feature_id, repo, branch, feature_yaml_path, project, status, triggered_by, originating_adapter, originating_user, correlation_id, parent_request_id, queued_at, started_at, completed_at, worktree_path, pr_url, error, max_turns, sdk_timeout_seconds) and adds the F4 recovery column `pending_approval_request_id TEXT`. The CREATE TABLE statement ends with the STRICT marker. test_schema_defines_strict_builds_table_with_pending_approval_column asserts presence of both the STRICT marker and the new column.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `schema.sql` contains `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stage_log (...) STRICT`
criteria_verification.evidence: schema.sql §2.2 declares stage_log STRICT with id (autoincrement), build_id FK, stage_label, target_kind+CHECK, target_identifier, status+CHECK, gate_mode+CHECK, coach_score, threshold_applied, started_at, completed_at, duration_secs, details_json — plus idx_stage_log_build and the partial idx_stage_log_gated index per API contract §2.2. Verified by test_schema_defines_stage_log_with_indices.
criteria_verification.notes: schema.sql §2.2 declares stage_log STRICT with id (autoincrement), build_id FK, stage_label, target_kind+CHECK, target_identifier, status+CHECK, gate_mode+CHECK, coach_score, threshold_applied, started_at, completed_at, duration_secs, details_json — plus idx_stage_log_build and the partial idx_stage_log_gated index per API contract §2.2. Verified by test_schema_defines_stage_log_with_indices.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `schema.sql` contains `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version` with
criteria_verification.evidence: schema.sql §2.3 declares the schema_version STRICT table and follows it with `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO schema_version (version, applied_at) VALUES (1, datetime('now'));`. test_schema_defines_schema_version_with_seed_row and test_apply_at_boot_creates_tables_and_seeds_schema_version both verify presence and runtime behaviour.
criteria_verification.notes: schema.sql §2.3 declares the schema_version STRICT table and follows it with `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO schema_version (version, applied_at) VALUES (1, datetime('now'));`. test_schema_defines_schema_version_with_seed_row and test_apply_at_boot_creates_tables_and_seeds_schema_version both verify presence and runtime behaviour.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `schema.sql` contains the UNIQUE INDEX on `(feature_id, correlation_id)`
criteria_verification.evidence: schema.sql defines `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_builds_feature_correlation ON builds (feature_id, correlation_id);`. test_schema_declares_unique_feature_correlation_index asserts the index name and UNIQUE marker; test_unique_feature_correlation_index_rejects_duplicates verifies enforcement at runtime by inserting a duplicate (feature_id, correlation_id) pair and asserting sqlite3.IntegrityError.
criteria_verification.notes: schema.sql defines `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_builds_feature_correlation ON builds (feature_id, correlation_id);`. test_schema_declares_unique_feature_correlation_index asserts the index name and UNIQUE marker; test_unique_feature_correlation_index_rejects_duplicates verifies enforcement at runtime by inserting a duplicate (feature_id, correlation_id) pair and asserting sqlite3.IntegrityError.

criteria_verification.evidence: src/forge/lifecycle/migrations.py implements apply_at_boot which (1) reads MAX(version) from schema_version, defaulting to 0 when the table does not yet exist, (2) skips already-applied migrations, and (3) wraps executescript in a `with connection:` transaction so failures roll back. The bundled schema.sql is itself idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + INSERT OR IGNORE). test_apply_at_boot_is_idempotent calls apply_at_boot twice and asserts schema_version still contains exactly one row [(1,)].
criteria_verification.notes: src/forge/lifecycle/migrations.py implements apply_at_boot which (1) reads MAX(version) from schema_version, defaulting to 0 when the table does not yet exist, (2) skips already-applied migrations, and (3) wraps executescript in a `with connection:` transaction so failures roll back. The bundled schema.sql is itself idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + INSERT OR IGNORE). test_apply_at_boot_is_idempotent calls apply_at_boot twice and asserts schema_version still contains exactly one row [(1,)].

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Both connection helpers apply the four mandatory pragmas (per DDR-003):
criteria_verification.evidence: src/forge/adapters/sqlite/connect.py defines _apply_pragmas() called from both connect_writer and read_only_connect. It sets journal_mode=WAL on the writer (read-only connections inherit WAL state and refuse the mutating form), and unconditionally sets synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000 on every open. test_connect_writer_applies_four_mandatory_pragmas reads back all four pragmas via PRAGMA round-trip and asserts the expected values (wal, 1, 1, 5000).
criteria_verification.notes: src/forge/adapters/sqlite/connect.py defines _apply_pragmas() called from both connect_writer and read_only_connect. It sets journal_mode=WAL on the writer (read-only connections inherit WAL state and refuse the mutating form), and unconditionally sets synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000 on every open. test_connect_writer_applies_four_mandatory_pragmas reads back all four pragmas via PRAGMA round-trip and asserts the expected values (wal, 1, 1, 5000).

criteria_verification.evidence: read_only_connect builds `uri = f"file:{db_path}?mode=ro"` and calls `sqlite3.connect(uri, uri=True, ...)`. test_read_only_connect_uses_mode_ro_uri_and_rejects_writes seeds a row via connect_writer, then opens via read_only_connect, reads the row back successfully, and asserts that an UPDATE raises sqlite3.OperationalError — proving SQLite engaged the read-only mode rather than just returning a plain connection.
criteria_verification.notes: read_only_connect builds `uri = f"file:{db_path}?mode=ro"` and calls `sqlite3.connect(uri, uri=True, ...)`. test_read_only_connect_uses_mode_ro_uri_and_rejects_writes seeds a row via connect_writer, then opens via read_only_connect, reads the row back successfully, and asserts that an UPDATE raises sqlite3.OperationalError — proving SQLite engaged the read-only mode rather than just returning a plain connection.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Integration test: open fresh DB, apply migrations, assert
criteria_verification.evidence: test_apply_migrations_then_journal_mode_is_wal opens a fresh DB in tmp_path via connect_writer, runs migrations.apply_at_boot, asserts PRAGMA journal_mode == 'wal' and SELECT version FROM schema_version == (1,). Passes (see Phase 4.5 transcript).
criteria_verification.notes: test_apply_migrations_then_journal_mode_is_wal opens a fresh DB in tmp_path via connect_writer, runs migrations.apply_at_boot, asserts PRAGMA journal_mode == 'wal' and SELECT version FROM schema_version == (1,). Passes (see Phase 4.5 transcript).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Integration test: STRICT enforcement — inserting a row with a non-INTEGER
criteria_verification.evidence: test_strict_table_rejects_non_integer_max_turns inserts a builds row with max_turns='not-an-int' and expects sqlite3.IntegrityError. The STRICT marker on the builds table is what causes SQLite to reject the value rather than silently coercing it. Passes.
criteria_verification.notes: test_strict_table_rejects_non_integer_max_turns inserts a builds row with max_turns='not-an-int' and expects sqlite3.IntegrityError. The STRICT marker on the builds table is what causes SQLite to reject the value rather than silently coercing it. Passes.

criteria_verification.evidence: test_wal_sidecar_files_appear_after_first_write opens connect_writer, applies migrations, executes an INSERT + commit, and (while the connection is still open, before SQLite's close-time checkpoint can collapse the WAL) asserts forge.db, forge.db-wal, and forge.db-shm all exist on disk. Passes.
criteria_verification.notes: test_wal_sidecar_files_appear_after_first_write opens connect_writer, applies migrations, executes an INSERT + commit, and (while the connection is still open, before SQLite's close-time checkpoint can collapse the WAL) asserts forge.db, forge.db-wal, and forge.db-shm all exist on disk. Passes.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with
criteria_verification.evidence: All new source files use type hints, snake_case names, PEP 8 line-lengths consistent with the existing forge.adapters.nats modules (88-char Black target), `from __future__ import annotations`, module-level docstrings, and structured Google-style docstrings on every public symbol. No project-level pre-commit/ruff/black config is wired into pyproject.toml in this worktree (only pytest config), so 'project-configured' lint amounts to the style guide in .claude/rules/code-style.md, which is satisfied. The pytest run completes with zero collection-time warnings.
criteria_verification.notes: All new source files use type hints, snake_case names, PEP 8 line-lengths consistent with the existing forge.adapters.nats modules (88-char Black target), `from __future__ import annotations`, module-level docstrings, and structured Google-style docstrings on every public symbol. issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)

rationale: All quality gates passed. Tests not required for this task type. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 376 chars provided.
context_used:
## Job-Specific Context

Budget: 1837/5200 tokens

### 🎨 Relevant Patterns
- ASSIGNS_TASK_TO
- USED_IN_CODE_PATTERN
- INSTRUCTS_USER_TO_START
- ENABLES_FEATURE
- HAS_BENEFIT

### ⚠️ Warnings
- DEPENDS_ON
- REQUIRES_USE_OF
- ERROR_CAUSE_OF

### 🎭 Role Constraints
- DELEGATE_TO_TASK_WORK
- HAS_ROLE_MUST_DO
- DELEGATES_TO

### 🛠️ Implementation Modes
- RESULT_LOCATION_PATTERN
